Klasse DatabaseManager

java.lang.Object
ch.framedev.lagersystem.managers.DatabaseManager
public class DatabaseManager extends Object
Simple DatabaseManager for SQLite.

Performance notes: - Avoids ParameterMetaData() (slow/unreliable in SQLite) - Adds pragmatic SQLite Pragmas (WAL, NORMAL sync, cache_size, mmap_size, busy_timeout, etc.) - Uses a small LRU cache for PreparedStatements (huge win for repeated queries/updates) - clearDatabase() uses a transaction and a single Statement for speed - clearTable() uses a strict whitelist (safe + fast)

    Modifikator und Typ
    Klasse

    static interface 
    DatabaseManager.ResultMapper<T>
    Functional interface for mapping a ResultSet row to an object of type T.

    • ALLOWED_TABLES

      public static final Set<String> ALLOWED_TABLES
      Whitelist of allowed table names for clearTable() to prevent SQL injection. Must be kept in sync with actual schema and clearTable() usage.

  • Konstruktordetails

    • DatabaseManager

      public DatabaseManager(String path, String fileName)
      Open (or create) the SQLite database at path/fileName.
      Parameter:
      path - directory path (maybe null or empty)
      fileName - database file name

  • Methodendetails

    • executeQuery

      @Deprecated public ResultSet executeQuery(String sql)
      Veraltet.
      Executes a raw SQL query string.

      SECURITY WARNING: Passing user-controlled input into this method can lead to SQL injection. Prefer executeQuery(String, Object...) (prepared statements) or the safe mapping helpers queryList(String, Object[], ResultMapper) / queryOne(String, Object[], ResultMapper).

      Parameter:
      sql - raw SQL query string (e.g. "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1")
      Gibt zurück:
      ResultSet or null on error; caller must call closeQuery(ResultSet) when done.

    • executeQuery

      public ResultSet executeQuery(String sql, Object... params)
      Executes a parameterized query using a cached PreparedStatement.

      Use SQL placeholders ("?") and pass parameters via varargs: 

          executeQuery("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?", username);

      Caller must call closeQuery(ResultSet).

      Parameter:
      sql - SQL string with "?" placeholders (e.g. "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?")
      params - parameters to bind to the placeholders (e.g. username)
      Gibt zurück:
      ResultSet or null on error; caller must call closeQuery(ResultSet) when done.

    • executePreparedQuery

      public ResultSet executePreparedQuery(String sql, Object[] params)
      Execute a prepared query and return the ResultSet. Caller must call closeQuery(rs).

      PreparedStatement is reused from an LRU cache.

      Parameter:
      sql - SQL string with "?" placeholders (e.g. "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?")
      params - parameters to bind to the placeholders (e.g. username)
      Gibt zurück:
      ResultSet or null on error; caller must call closeQuery(ResultSet) when done.

    • executePreparedUpdate

      public boolean executePreparedUpdate(String sql, Object[] params)
      Execute prepared update statement.

      PreparedStatement is reused from an LRU cache.

      Parameter:
      sql - SQL string with "?" placeholders (e.g. "UPDATE users SET last_login = ? WHERE id = ?")
      params - parameters to bind to the placeholders (e.g. lastLogin, userId)
      Gibt zurück:
      true if update succeeded, false on error

    • closeQuery

      public void closeQuery(ResultSet rs)
      Close the given ResultSet and its creating Statement (if tracked).
      Parameter:
      rs - ResultSet to close (maybe null)

    • closeAllOpenQueries

      public void closeAllOpenQueries()
      Close all currently tracked open queries (ResultSets and their Statements).

    • executeUpdate

      @Deprecated public boolean executeUpdate(String sql)
      Veraltet.
      Executes a raw SQL update/DDL string.

      SECURITY WARNING: Passing user-controlled input into this method can lead to SQL injection. Prefer executeUpdate(String, Object...) (prepared statements).

      Parameter:
      sql - raw SQL update/DDL string (e.g. "UPDATE users SET last_login = '2024-01-01' WHERE id = 1")
      Gibt zurück:
      true if update succeeded, false on error

    • executeUpdate

      public boolean executeUpdate(String sql, Object... params)
      Executes a parameterized update/DDL using a cached PreparedStatement.

      Use SQL placeholders ("?") and pass parameters via varargs: 

          executeUpdate("UPDATE users SET last_login = ? WHERE id = ?", lastLogin, userId);
      Parameter:
      sql - SQL string with "?" placeholders (e.g. "UPDATE users SET last_login = ? WHERE id = ?")
      params - parameters to bind to the placeholders (e.g. lastLogin, userId)
      Gibt zurück:
      true if update succeeded, false on error

    • executeUpdateWithCount

      @Deprecated public int executeUpdateWithCount(String sql)
      Veraltet.
      Executes a raw SQL update/DDL string and returns affected rows count (or -1 on error).

      SECURITY WARNING: Passing user-controlled input into this method can lead to SQL injection. Prefer executeUpdateWithCount(String, Object...) (prepared statements).

      Gibt zurück:
      affected rows count, or -1 if an error occurs

    • executeUpdateWithCount

      public int executeUpdateWithCount(String sql, Object... params)
      Executes a parameterized update/DDL and returns affected rows count (or -1 on error).
      Parameter:
      sql - SQL string with "?" placeholders (e.g. "UPDATE users SET last_login = ? WHERE id = ?")
      params - parameters to bind to the placeholders (e.g. lastLogin, user, Id)
      Gibt zurück:
      affected rows count, or -1 if an error occurs

    • getConnection

      public Connection getConnection()
      Expose raw Connection if needed.
      Gibt zurück:
      JDBC Connection

    • close

      public void close()
      Close manager: close tracked queries, close cached statements, close connection.

    • clearDatabase

      public boolean clearDatabase()
      Clear all data from the database. WARNING: destructive operation that removes ALL data from ALL tables. Uses a transaction to ensure atomicity (all or nothing).
      Gibt zurück:
      true if all tables were cleared successfully, false if any error occurs (in which case no data will be deleted due to rollback).

    • clearTable

      public boolean clearTable(String tableName)
      Clear a specific table from the database.

      IMPORTANT: You cannot bind table names with PreparedStatement placeholders. Therefore we whitelist table names and build SQL directly.

      Parameter:
      tableName - name of the table to clear (must be in ALLOWED_TABLES)
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the table was cleared successfully, false if the table name is invalid or an error occurs.

    • queryList

      public <T> List<T> queryList(String sql, Object[] params, DatabaseManager.ResultMapper<T> mapper)
      Helper for list-result queries. Returns a list of mapped objects, or an empty list on error.
      Typparameter:
      T - the type of the mapped objects
      Parameter:
      sql - SQL string with "?" placeholders (e.g. "SELECT * FROM users WHERE department = ?")
      params - parameters to bind to the placeholders (e.g. departmentName)
      mapper - function to map each ResultSet row to an object of type T
      Gibt zurück:
      list of mapped objects of type T, or an empty list if an error occurs

    • queryOne

      public <T> T queryOne(String sql, Object[] params, DatabaseManager.ResultMapper<T> mapper)
      Helper for single-result queries. Returns the mapped object or null if no results.
      Typparameter:
      T - the type of the mapped object
      Parameter:
      sql - SQL string with "?" placeholders (e.g. "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?")
      params - parameters to bind to the placeholders (e.g. userId)
      mapper - function to map the ResultSet row to an object of type T
      Gibt zurück:
      mapped object of type T or null if no results

    • inTransaction

      public boolean inTransaction(Consumer<Connection> work)
      Helper for executing multiple operations in a transaction. If any exception occurs, the transaction is rolled back.
      Parameter:
      work - a Consumer that accepts a Connection and performs the desired operations; it can throw exceptions to trigger rollback
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the transaction was committed successfully, false if it was rolled back due to an exception
